paper.exam: B.Tech./B.Tech. (Working Professional), IV Semester Examination, December 2025
paper.note:
i) Attempt any five questions.
ii) All questions carry equal marks.
iii) Assume any missing data.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Differentiate between File System and DBMS. Discuss the advantages of a database system over a file-based system.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Draw and explain an E-R diagram for a University database system. Consider entities like students, courses and faculty.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Explain the concept of weak entity sets. How do generalization, specialization and aggregation extend the E-R model.

parts.label: b
parts.text:
Consider the following relational schema:
Student(Roll_no, Name, Dept, Year, Marks)
write relational algebra queries to:
i) Retrieve names of students in the CS department
ii) Find students who scored more than 80 marks

parts.label: a
parts.text: Compare sorting, join and selection operations in query processing.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Why is query optimization necessary in large databases? Discuss with an example. Explain the measures of query cost.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Discuss the types of anomalies that arise in an un-normalized database. Explain how normalization helps to eliminate these anomalies.

parts.label: b
parts.text:
Consider the following relation.
Employee (Emp_ID, Name, Dept, Salary, Project_ID, Project_Name, Manager_ID)
i) Identify functional dependencies
ii) Convert it to 3NF and BCNF.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Define a transaction in a database. Explain the ACID properties of transactions with examples.

parts.label: b
parts.text: Why is concurrency control important in database systems? Discuss the impact of concurrency control on transaction performance.

parts.label: a
parts.text: What is data independence? Explain logical and physical data independence with an example.

parts.label: b
parts.text: How does relational algebra help in database query processing? Explain with an example.

parts.label: a
parts.text: Explain the importance of decomposition in database design. What is lossless and dependency-preserving decomposition?

parts.label: b
parts.text: What is deadlock in a database? Explain deadlock prevention and deadlock detection mechanisms.

Write short notes on (any two)
a) Two-Phase Locking (2PL) protocol (Unit 5)
b) Distributed databases: Concepts and Challenges (Unit 5)
c) Serializability in transactions (Unit 5)
d) Multi-valued dependency (Unit 4)
